turns.student: Hi, I'm here to help you. What's been going on?
turns.patient: I've been experiencing severe abdominal pain, mainly on my left side, and it's been quite distressing. The pain is accompanied by bloody stools with mucus, which has become a significant concern for me.

turns.student: That sounds really uncomfortable. I can tell this has been weighing on you.
turns.patient: It's been affecting every aspect of my life - work, sleep, even my relationships. The worst part is that I've tried to self-medicate with loperamide, but it hasn't provided any significant relief, and I'm starting to worry about what might be causing these symptoms.

turns.student: I want to make sure we don't miss anything important. When these episodes happen, does your body react in any other way?
turns.patient: The diarrhea has become so frequent that I've gone from having 1-2 bowel movements a day to 8-10, and it's not just loose stools, but also watery with a sense of urgency. The pain is crampy, and sometimes I get this feeling of incomplete evacuation after most bowel movements, which makes me feel really uncomfortable.

turns.student: I know it can be scary to think about, but do you notice any sweating or feeling faint during these episodes?
turns.patient: Actually, no, I don't experience those symptoms. But what's concerning is that I've lost about 4 kg (9 lbs) over the past three weeks due to the diarrhea and vomiting, which has affected my energy levels and work performance significantly.

turns.student: Thank you for sharing that. I really appreciate your honesty. Does the discomfort stay in one place or does it spread anywhere?
turns.patient: The pain primarily stays on my left side, but sometimes it radiates to my lower back and knees, which gets achy too, especially after a long day at work.

turns.student: It must be frightening when symptoms like these happen. Any other areas of your body affected?
turns.patient: Yes, I also experience fatigue that worsens with the diarrhea, and I've noticed a low-grade fever a few times per week. And to make matters worse, my work performance has been suffering because of this, and I'm really worried about what might be causing these symptoms, especially since I haven't seen a doctor in over 2 years.

turns.student: I'm concerned about you. Have you noticed any swelling anywhere? Even minor changes?
turns.patient: No, I don't have any noticeable swelling on my body, including my knees and ankles where I sometimes get achy pain. The joint pain is more of a dull ache than anything else.

turns.student: Sometimes when people are going through something like this, they also feel nauseous or have stomach upset. Has that happened to you?
turns.patient: To be honest, yes, I've experienced some nausea and vomiting episodes, especially after eating certain foods or when my symptoms worsen. It's made me really cautious about what I eat, but even that doesn't always help.

turns.student: I can see this is taking a toll on you. How has your energy been? Your appetite?
turns.patient: My energy levels have been significantly affected - I used to be able to handle long work hours without issues, but now even small tasks feel exhausting. As for my appetite, it's irregular due to my busy schedule and stress from these symptoms, so I mostly rely on takeout or easy meals, which isn't great for my overall health.

turns.student: Have you had any fevers or night sweats? I know it might seem unrelated, but it helps me understand the full picture.
turns.patient: Yes, I've had a low-grade fever, usually around 37.8-38.0°C, a few times per week. It's not extremely high, but it's still concerning to me.

turns.student: Your health really matters to me as your care provider. Can you tell me about any other medical conditions you've been diagnosed with?
turns.patient: I actually had an appendectomy at age 16, and that was a few decades ago, so I don't think it's related to my current symptoms. Other than that, I'm pretty healthy, or at least I thought I was until these issues started showing up.

turns.student: I know medications can be a hassle. Are you taking everything as prescribed, or has anything been hard to keep up with?
turns.patient: To be honest, I've been self-medicating with loperamide (Imodium) for diarrhea over the past 2 weeks, but it hasn't provided any significant relief, which is why I'm really worried about what might be causing these symptoms.

turns.student: I don't want to pry, but understanding your lifestyle really helps me help you. How do you eat on a typical day?
turns.patient: My eating habits have been pretty irregular due to my long work hours and stress from these symptoms. I usually grab takeout or easy meals because it's hard for me to find time to cook healthy food, which isn't ideal but it's what works best for my schedule right now.

turns.student: It sounds like your life keeps you really busy. Do you get a chance to exercise or stay active?
turns.patient: Exercise has actually been one of the things I've struggled with lately due to the fatigue and abdominal discomfort, but before all this started, I was pretty active - I used to go for runs or do weightlifting on most days. Now, even short walks feel like a lot of effort, which is really frustrating because I know exercise can help with stress and energy levels, but it's just not something I've been able to do much of lately.

turns.student: I hate to ask this, but it's really important — has anyone in your family had serious health problems?
turns.patient: My older sister was diagnosed with Crohn's disease at age 30. It's been a bit concerning for me because of the similarity in symptoms and the fact that I haven't seen a doctor in over two years.

turns.student: I'm so sorry to hear that. That must have been incredibly difficult. Do you worry about your own health because of it?
turns.patient: Yes, I do worry about my own health, especially with these symptoms and the family history. It's hard not to think about the possibility of something serious being wrong, given what happened to my sister. The uncertainty is really unsettling for me.

turns.student: Given everything you've been through, I want to be thorough. Do you drink alcohol? How do you cope with stress?
turns.patient: Yes, I do drink alcohol - 2-3 beers most weeknights have become my usual, but over the past month, I've actually increased it to 4-5 per night due to stress from symptoms and work. It's been a coping mechanism for me, but I know it's not ideal, especially with how these symptoms have been affecting my life.

turns.student: I want to make sure we're being thorough. Do you smoke or use any substances?
turns.patient: No, I don't smoke and I don't use any recreational drugs. The stress from these symptoms and work has actually made me more cautious about my habits, so I've been trying to cut back on everything that might be exacerbating the issue.

turns.student: Is there anything you've been hesitant to tell me? I promise there's no judgment here.
turns.patient:
I think what's been holding me back from sharing some things is my fear of what it might mean for my health. But if it'll help get to the bottom of this, I should probably mention that my father has type 2 diabetes and my mother is healthy. And also, I've recently increased my work hours at the startup where I'm a software engineer, which has been adding to my stress levels, especially with the long hours and high expectations.

Additionally, I did travel to Mexico for a work conference about three months ago, but I had no GI issues during or after the trip. And as for sick contacts, I don't have any known exposure to anyone with similar symptoms, nor have I experienced food poisoning recently.

Lastly, it might seem unrelated, but my living situation is pretty solitary - I live alone in an apartment here in Austin.

turns.student: Before we wrap up, is there anything else you're worried about that you want to share?
turns.patient: I'm worried that something serious might be wrong with me. The uncertainty and lack of answers are really getting to me. I feel like I've been trying everything on my own, from self-medicating to changing my diet, but nothing seems to be working. That's why I'm so desperate for someone to listen and help me figure out what's going on.
